description_long: This is a large commercial jet airliner with a predominantly white fuselage accented by bold red geometric patterns along the body and tail. The aircraft features a wide-body design with four turbofan engines mounted under its low, swept-back wings. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is located at the forward tip of the fuselage, with a distinctive multi-window arrangement typical of passenger jets. The landing gear is not visible in these images, suggesting it may be retracted. The overall design and proportions are consistent with a long-range, high-capacity passenger airplane.
description: A large, white and red commercial jet airliner with four underwing engines, swept low wings, and a conventional tail. The cockpit is forward-mounted with multiple windows.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, four engines, and the red and white pattern along the fuselage and tail.
description_view_1: Left side profile highlighting the low, swept wings, engine placement, and the geometric red markings on the fuselage and tail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the symmetry of the wings and engines.
description_view_3: Right side profile similar to view 1, showing the opposite side's red and white pattern and engine arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with long, swept-back wings and no visible winglets; standard dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing turbofan engines, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear (not visible, inferred from typical design).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, typical of multi-seat commercial airliners.
